channel.title: Port on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Port on TLDRBins

item.title: Network Discovery
item.description: Test Connectivity ping -c3 10.10.11.10 Test Reverse Connectivity sudo tcpdump -ni tun0 icmp Sniff Network Traffic # For example: on port 389 tcpdump -i lo -nnXs 0 'port 389' Use ping to Scan Subnet for i in $(seq 1 254); do (ping -c 1 10.100.10.${i} | grep "bytes from" &); done; Use nc to Scan Ports for i in $(seq 1 65535); do (nc -zvn 127.0.0.1 ${i} 2>&1 | grep -v "Connection refused" &); done Check ARP arp -na cat /proc/net/arp Check IP # Linux ifconfig ip addr cat /proc/net/fib_trie # Windows ipconfig Check Network Connections # TCP netstat -plant # UDP netstat -plunt # If netstat not present cat /proc/net/tcp | grep '00000000:0000 0A' # Windows netstat -ano

item.title: Nmap
item.description: Nmap Port Scan TCP Scan target=10.10.11.10 nmap -p- --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-alltcp $target ports=$(cat nmap-alltcp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sCV -oA ./nmap-tcpscripts $target UDP Scan (common ports) target=10.10.11.10 nmap -sU --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-udp $target ports=$(cat nmap-udp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sU -sCV -oA ./nmap-udpscripts $target Scan with nmap scripts Update scripts DB nmap --script-updatedb Search scripts nmap --script-help ftp* Scan with a script nmap --script=ftp-anon -sV -sC -p 21 10.
